key_insight: A matrix-free, high-order phase-field fracture solver can efficiently capture steep crack damage gradients while preserving inelastic constitutive behavior in micromechanical simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables more scalable GPU-based fracture analysis of particle-matrix materials under tensile and compressive loading, which is valuable for predictive micromechanics and damage-tolerant design.
method: Implemented the Peric & Dettmer visco-elastoplastic constitutive framework in an open-source solid mechanics library.
Used matrix-free operators with high-order finite elements and p-multigrid preconditioning on GPUs.
Added a rheological fracture element in series to separate inelastic and fracture effects at homogenization scales.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated through numerical tensile and compressive simulations of synthetic particle-matrix microstructures on an El Capitan prototype, reproducing characteristic inelastic responses and crack propagation patterns.
watch_item: High-order, matrix-free phase-field simulations can be sensitive to mesh/element choices and preconditioning quality, especially when resolving steep crack gradients on GPUs.
paper_title: Matrix-free phase-field modeling of fracture in micromechanical testing simulations of inelastic materials
